title: T-005 — sites_hierarchy_config + site_capacity tables (§9.4, §9.5, D-MS-10)
prompt:
# T-005 — sites_hierarchy_config + site_capacity tables

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §9.4, §9.5, D-MS-10
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Add the two ancillary core tables: `sites_hierarchy_config` (one row per tenant defining hierarchy depth 2-5 and level_names per ADR-030) and `site_capacity` (per-site per-date available/utilized hours for P2B planning).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0044_hierarchy_capacity.sql`:
   - CREATE TABLE sites_hierarchy_config per §9.5: id, org_id UNIQUE, depth INT CHECK (BETWEEN 2 AND 5), level_names TEXT[] NOT NULL with CHECK (array_length(level_names,1) = depth), description, created_at. RLS org-scoped.
   - CREATE TABLE site_capacity per §9.4: id, org_id, site_id (FK sites), capacity_date, line_id NULL, available_hours NUMERIC(6,2) CHECK (>=0), utilized_hours NUMERIC(6,2) DEFAULT 0 CHECK (>=0), notes, UNIQUE(org_id, site_id, capacity_date, line_id). RLS site-scoped (via app.current_site_id()).
2. Drizzle schemas + zod with level_names length === depth refinement.
3. Foreign key sites.hierarchy_config_id → sites_hierarchy_config(id) added in this migration (DDL is forward-ref in T-002; this completes the cycle without altering T-002).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0044_hierarchy_capacity.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/sites-hierarchy-config.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/site-capacity.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/hierarchy-capacity.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0044 applied, when \d sites_hierarchy_config inspected, then depth CHECK (2-5) + array_length(level_names) = depth CHECK both enforced, org_id UNIQUE.
2. Given an insert with depth=3 and level_names=['site','plant'], when committed, then DB raises check_violation (length mismatch).
3. Given migration 0044 applied, when \d site_capacity inspected, then UNIQUE(org_id,site_id,capacity_date,line_id) + available_hours/utilized_hours CHECK (>=0) present + RLS+FORCE RLS enabled with site-scoped policy.
4. Given two sites in same org, when app_user under withSiteContext(siteId=X) queries site_capacity, then only X's rows are returned (RLS isolation).
5. Given pg_policies for site_capacity, when inspected, then policy references app.current_site_id() (site-scoped variant).

## Test strategy
- RED: assert level_names length check + depth CHECK + RLS site-scoped isolation + capacity CHECK constraints + pg_policies grep.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- hierarchy-capacity'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not allow array_length mismatch — DB CHECK constraint must enforce (Postgres array_length on 1D array).
- Do not org-scope site_capacity — it must be site-scoped per §6.4.
- Do not skip FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
- Do not couple Apex seed data to this migration — seed in T-023 activation wizard.

Given an insert with depth=3 and level_names=['site','plant'], when committed, then DB raises check_violation (length mismatch).

Given migration 0044 applied, when \\d site_capacity inspected, then UNIQUE(org_id,site_id,capacity_date,line_id) + available_hours/utilized_hours CHECK (>=0) present + RLS+FORCE RLS enabled with site-scoped policy.

Given two sites in same org, when app_user under withSiteContext(siteId=X) queries site_capacity, then only X's rows are returned; AND given pg_policies for site_capacity when inspected then policy references app.current_site_id() (site-scoped variant, no raw GUC reads).
